decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.2°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective and safe lever is to maximize the heat spreader conductivity, which was previously attempted at an invalid value (1000.0). I will set it to its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K) to improve lateral heat spreading without violating geometric or material constraints.

changes.reason: Maximize thermal conductivity of the spreader to enhance lateral heat distribution and reduce peak chip temperature. Note: The current state already shows 500.0, but previous logs indicate attempts to exceed this. Confirming the bound is the correct physical limit; if the simulation state reflects a lower effective value due to prior errors, this ensures the parameter is correctly pinned to the maximum allowed.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
Improved feasibility regarding the chip_max_temperature constraint
risk_notes: If the conductivity is already at 500.0 in the solver's actual memory despite the JSON showing it, this change may have no effect, requiring a geometric adjustment (width/height) in the next step.
Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
